observations.recommendation: 5. The Inventory Committee plan/strategize on how to conduct and complete the physical inventory within the prescribed period. It shall prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) duly approved by the Mayor, containing the specific assignments/duties of the Committee members, the cut-off date, and a schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory taking activities from start-up to the targeted completion of physical inventory;
6. The Inventory Committee submit the approved PIP to the COA Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory; and

7. Property records will be updated by the General Services Officer based on the result of the physical count and reconciled with accounting records to come up with the correct balances of PPE accounts.
9. General Service Officer – Keep a complete Property Card for all the infrastructure assets and maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map;
10. Municipal Accountant – Prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card;
11. Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures on the corresponding accounts of the Local Road Network and the Water Supply System.
12. Keep and maintain subsidiary records of roads and road components and the water supply system;
13. Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each infrastructure account at the end of the year.

14. We recommended that Management create a special account in the General Fund (SAGF) for the BGCM Fund through an ordinance by the local Sanggunian under Section 313 of RA No. 7160 to easily identify and distinguish the utilization of COVID-19 related funds.
15. We recommended that the Management prepare a monthly report on BGCM fund utilization and the status of implementation of PPAs under DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125.

16. We recommended that the Honorable Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers related to the BGMC fund to avoid receipt of notices of suspension and disallowances.
20. Finally, confirm, reconcile, and remit the prior year’s balances to the respective GOCCs if warranted.
25.The Municipal Mayor, being the head of the agency institutionalizes the ICS;
26. Management to compel the Municipal Officers to act and do their respective duties and functions strictly by-laws, rules, and regulations; and
27. Management to faithfully adhere to the application of internal control measures for the efficient and effective conduct of the operation of the Municipality towards development.

38. We recommended that Management furnish a COA copy of any contract/purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 within 5-days after its perfection/ approval/execution for proper evaluation and remedial measures on possible defects will not be too late to enforce.

1.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Permanently appoint a competent General Services Officer /
Property Officer who shall be responsible with the PPE related
functions and records;
b) Mandate the concerned personnel to conduct of a thorough and
complete physical count of all properties of the Municipality; and
c) Require the submission of a complete report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant, and Equipment (RCPPE).

a) The Accounting Office to record and account for the purchase of
welfare goods, medicines, and other supplies and materials in a
perpetual inventory method;
b) The GSO and Accounting Office to implement internal control
measures to ensure that inventories are safeguarded from
possible wastage and misappropriation; and
c) The GSO to diligently prepare, maintain and update inventory
records, forms, and reports, and consequently reconcile such
with the ledgers of the Accounting Office.

3.7 Thus, we recommended that the LCE mandate the concerned officials
to faithfully observe the following courses of action in the preparation
and submission of the financial reports and statements, viz:
a) Strictly adopt the prescribed templates provided in the NGAS
Manual for LGU, Volumes I and II;
b) Strictly indicate the necessary information and data on the financial
reports/statement such as detailed description, unit measure, report
number, and/or remarks; and
c) Strictly reproduce the required number of copies, for each type of
report, and furnish the same to the concerned offices.

4.8 We recommended that the Honorable Mayor mandate that cash
advances be strictly monitored, liquidated, and properly booked within
the prescribed period and reported to the Municipal Accountant as
soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served.

5.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer must affect a deduction of accurate mandatory contributions
to GSIS, PhilHealth, Pag-ibig, and withheld taxes and further remit
them within the prescribed period of remittances. The prior year’s
accounts must be reconciled, confirmed, and remitted, if not, then
initiate the applicable alternative action on that matter.

6.4 We recommended that Management:
a) Before payment of claims, continue the strict adherence to COA
Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on submission of
documentary requirements needed on common government
transactions to avoid suspensions and/or disallowances in audit;
and
b) Exhaust all available remedies provided under existing laws, rules,
and regulations in enforcing immediate settlement of suspensions
and disallowances (if any) under Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No.
2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

1. The LCE directs the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and document the obligations and accounts paid with checks by preparing the RCI in chronological order and including the supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant;

2. The Municipal Accountant shall see to it that the RCI is duly certified by the Municipal Treasurer and check that the attached supporting documents are complete before issuance of JEV and recording thereof in the Check Disbursement Journal; and

3. Finally, the original copies of the RCI, together with the Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents shall then be submitted to the Office of the Auditor within the time frame prescribed by laws , rules, and regulations.

4. The LCE immediately creates the Inventory Committee, composed of an adequate number of members, and complete the physical count in three (3) months or less and submit a report thereon. The Inventory Committee shall have at least one member each from the Accounting and Property Divisions/Units of the agency;

8. Municipal Engineer – Provide the local accountant and the general service officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects and the water supply system;

17. We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Planning Development Officer to prepare the supplemental AIP for the COVID-19-related PPAs and the corresponding supplemental Budget approved by the local Sanggunian in support of the utilization of the BGCM fund.
18. Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to effect an accurate deduction of mandatory contributions from the employees’ compensation and procurement transactions;
19. Thereafter, remit the same to GSIS, PhilHealth, Pag-ibig, and BIR within the prescribed period; and

21. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor must instruct and supervise the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update these registries as per provisions outlined in Sections 18 to 22, Volume II of Manual on NGAS for LGUs.
22. Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer an Assessment Roll containing the list of all persons whose real properties have been assessed for RPT;
23. Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Chief Accountant with a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and
24. Municipal Accountant to properly establish and set up the RPT/SET Receivable accounts due and collectible for the year.
28. Full disclosure policy be exercised on the PPA’s for the 20% development fund and other funds for transparency and accountability purposes;
29. At the start of the year, the list of all ongoing government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year, with complete details, be submitted; and
30. Submission of the monthly monitoring report on the status of PPA’s on 20% development fund be regularized.

31. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRMO to prepare and submit the annual MDRRMFIP for the projects and activities listed in approved plans including those projects intended for the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in compliance with section 5.1.2 of COA Circular 2012-002.
32. We recommended that GAD funds be judicially and efficiently utilized and implemented to achieve the GAD Program objectives and gender mainstream.

33. We further recommended that GAD required reports for monitoring and evaluation must be duly prepared and furnished to COA in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

34. Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms must be submitted for proper validation, easier tracking of the used and unused accountable forms, and avoid possible loss and misuse of the accountable forms.

35. The serial number of checks issued must be reported and arranged chronologically in the RCI, Cashbook-Cash in Ba, and Statement of Accountability for Accountable Forms and other reports for proper accounting and easier validation of documents and monitoring of the existence of the accountable forms.

36. We recommended that Management direct the treatment of travel expenses as cash advances if paid before the date of travel and liquidations and/or reimbursement must be attached with complete supporting documents.

37. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive institute the creation of a Local School Board (LSB) in his locality and then required the constituted LSB for the preparation of the Annual School Budget Board for purposes of proper utilization and disbursement of Special Education Fund (SEF) in compliance with Section 98 of RA 7160 and Item Nos. 4 and 5.2 of DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.

1. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor must instruct and
supervise the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update these
registries as per provisions set-forth in Sections 18 to 22, Volume II
of Manual on NGAS for LGUs.

2. Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit to the Municipal
Treasurer an Assessment Roll containing the list of all persons
whose real properties have been assessed for RPT;
3. Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Chief Accountant of a duly
certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due
and collectible for the year; and
4. Municipal Accountant to properly establish and set-up the
RPT/SET Receivable accounts due and collectible for the year.

5. The Municipal Mayor, being the head of the agency institutionalize the
ICS;
6. Management to compel the Municipal Officers to act and do their respective
duties and functions strictly in accordance with laws, rules and regulations; and
7. Management to faithfully adhere to the application of internal control
measures for an efficient and effective conduct of the operation of the
Municipality towards development.

8. Full disclosure policy be exercised on the PPA’s for the 20% development fund and
other funds for transparency and accountability purpose;
9. At the start of the year, the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to
be implemented during the year, with complete details, be submitted; and
10. Submission of the monthly monitoring report on the status of PPA’s on 20% development
fund be regularized.

11. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRMO to prepare and
submit the annual MDRRMFIP for the projects and activities listed in approved plans
including those projects intended for the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in
compliance with section 5.1.2 of COA Circular 2012-002.

12. We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive must require
the Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended/ unobligated balance of MDRRF to
STF and proper utilization of the MDRRF fund in accordance with MDRRMFIP per COA
Circular 2012-002.

13. We recommended that GAD funds be judicially and efficiently utilized and implemented to
achieve the GAD Program objectives and gender mainstream.
14. We further recommended that GAD required reports for monitoring and evaluation must be
duly prepared and furnished COA in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2013-01.

15. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer effect an accurate
deduction of the mandatory contributions of GSIS, PhilHealth, Pag-ibig, and withheld taxes, and
remit them within prescribed period of remittances.

16. Management must keep intact the Requisition and Issue Vouchers including the logbook records
wherein all the accountable forms are listed and the name of person/AO who shall be accountable.
This is also done for proper booking the value of the accountable forms and accounting the
issuances and releases.

17. We recommended that the Accountable Officer must submit the original copy of the cancelled
checks and/or Official Receipt in the Report of Checks Issued and/or Report of Collections and
Deposits for proper validation of cancelled forms.

18. Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms must be regularly submitted for proper
validation, easier tracking of the used and unused accountable forms and avoid possible loss and
misuses of the accountable forms.

19. The serial number of checks issued must be reported and arranged in chronological manner in the
RCI, Cashbook-Cash in Bank, and Statement of Accountability for Accountable Forms and other
reports for proper accounting and easier validation of documents and monitoring the existence of the
accountable forms.

20. We recommended that Management ensure payment of casuals/contractual supported verified/accepted
accomplishment reports and appointments are submitted to CSC and together with other documentary
Requirements in accordance to Section 4.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012- 001 dated June 14, 2012.

21. We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper reporting
and keeping of accounts and maintain the General and subsidiary ledgers not only on revenue accounts
but on all accounts presented in the Financial Statements in compliance with Sections 111, 112 and 114
(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

22. We recommended that Management direct the treatment of travel expenses as cash advances if paid
before the date of travel and liquidations and/ or reimbursement must be attached with complete
supporting documents.

25. We recommended that Management furnish COA copy of any contract/purchase order including all
pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 within 5- days after its
perfection/ approval/execution for proper evaluation and remedial measures on possible defects will not
be too late to enforce.

26. We recommend that the accountant thoroughly process, check the computation of payrolls and require
all concerned official and employee to attach the individual daily time record including the other prescribed
documentary requirements before payment is made.
27. For effective internal control of handling salaries, it was further recommended that payment of salaries
must be done through Automotive Teller Machine (ATM) to lessen the grant of cash advances and avoid the
possibility of payroll padding.

29. We recommended that the management ensure notification to the Auditor of the time and date of every
scheduled delivery of goods and services be made on time pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May
18, 1995.

30. We recommended that management make sure that observers (consisting of the concerned auditor, a
representative of a duly recognized private group in a sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand
and a representative of NGO) are duly notified in writing at least 2-day before the stages of procurement to
which observers shall be invited, namely: pre-bid conference; opening of bids; post-qualification; contract
award; and special meetings of the BAC.

31. Must initiate to obtain internet access to facilitate internet publication of procurements, such as
inviting possible internet providers to install internet services in the community.
32. Make some pre-arrangements with newspaper media of general nationwide circulation in a way that
could facilitate newspaper publications incurring less costs, time and efforts for the LGU, i.e. through phone
call, text message or e-mail.
33. Provide campaign strategies designed at promoting positive image of the Municipality where peace and
order is stable and people are hospitable.

34. Must made sure that the conditions and requirements prescribed in Appendix I of RA 9184 are duly
complied with prior to the implementation/construction of any project to be undertaken by administration;
35. Must provide campaign strategies designed at promoting positive image of the Municipality where peace
and order is stable and people are hospitable; and
36. Install safety and security measures so that the life and property of any prospective contractors can be
safeguarded.

37. The Municipal Due Accountant must immediately submit the paid vouchers and related payrolls pertaining
to the remittances made during the year and to establish the amount withheld from salaries of employees and
as required per Sec. 100 of P.D. 1445.

1.6 We therefore recommended that:
a) The LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and
document the obligations and accounts paid with checks by
preparing the RCI in chronological order and submit the same
within the prescribed period including the supporting documents
to the Municipal Accountant;
b) The Municipal Accountant shall see to it that the RCI is duly
certified by the Municipal Treasurer and check that the attached
supporting documents are complete prior issuance of JEV and
recording thereof in the Check Disbursement Journal; and
c) Finally, the original copies of the RCI, together with the
Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents shall then be
submitted to the Office of the Auditor within time frame
prescribed by laws rules and regulations.

2.4 We therefore recommended that:
a) The LCE immediately create the Inventory Committee, composed
of adequate number of members, and complete the physical count
in three (3) months or less and submit a report thereon. The
Inventory Committee shall have at least one member each from
the Accounting and Property Divisions/Units of the agency;
b) The Inventory Committee plan/strategize on how to conduct and
complete the physical inventory within the prescribed period. It
shall prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) duly approved by the
Mayor, containing the specific assignments/duties of the
Committee members, the cut-off date and a schedule specifying
the dates and locations of the inventory taking activities from start
up to the targeted completion of physical inventory;
c) The Inventory Committee submit the approved PIP to the COA
Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the schedule
start of inventory; and
d) Property records will be updated by the General Services Officer
based on the result of the physical count and reconciled with
accounting records to come up with correct balances of PPE
accounts.

3.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following to the
concerned Municipal Officials:
a) Municipal Engineer – Provide the local accountant and the general service
officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects and the water supply system;
b) General Service Officer – Keep a complete Property Card to all the
infrastructure assets and maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map;
c) Municipal Accountant –
i. Prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record the beginning balance of
the local road network and its components in the general ledger and
the Local Road Network Ledger Card;
ii. Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures on the
corresponding accounts of Local Road Network and the Water Supply
System. Work on the distribution of cost for road components and the
water supply system and determine the corresponding depreciated
replacement cost for road components with no available cost per
registry;
iii. Keep and maintain subsidiary records to roads and road components
and the water supply system;
iv. Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for
each infrastructures account at the end of the year.

4.6 We recommended that Management create a special account in the
General Fund (SAGF) for the BGCM Fund through an ordinance by
the local Sanggunian pursuant to Section 313 of RA No. 7160 to easily
identify and distinguish the utilization of COVID-19 related funds.

5.5 We recommended that Management prepare a monthly report on
BGCM fund utilization and status of implementation of PPAs pursuant
to DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125.

6.4 We recommended that Honorable Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the
disbursement vouchers related to the BGMC fund to avoid receipt of
notices of suspension and disallowances.

7.4 We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Budget Officer
and the Municipal Planning Development Officer to prepare the
supplemental AIP for the COVID-19-related PPAs and the
corresponding supplemental Budget approved by the local Sanggunian
in support to the utilization of BGCM fund.

8.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) To instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer
effect an accurate deduction of an mandatory contributions from the
employees’ compensation and procurement transactions;
b) Thereafter, remit the same to GSIS, PhilHealth, Pag-ibig, and BIR
within prescribed period.; and
c) Finally, confirm, reconcile, and remit the prior year balances to the
respective GOCCs if warranted.

9.2 We recommended that:
a) Prior to payment of claims, Management must strictly comply
with the documentary requirements needed on common
government transactions as embodied in COA Circular 2012-001
dated June 14, 2012, to avoid suspensions and/or disallowances in
audit; and
b) Exhaust all available remedies provided under existing laws, rules
and regulations in enforcing immediate settlement of the
disallowances and suspensions pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA
Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

23. We recommended that the Municipal Government determine their insurable assets and adequately
cover it with GSIS insurance to indemnify the assets in case of damage/loss due to fire, earthquake and
another fortuitous event.

24. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive institute the creation of Local School Board (LSB) in
his locality and then required the constituted LSB for the preparation of Annual School Budget Board for
purposes of proper utilization and disbursement of Special Education Fund (SEF) in compliance with
Section 98 of RA 7160 and Item Nos. 4 and 5.2 of DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.

38. The LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit the required report at
soonest time and strictly adhere with Section 100 of P.D. 1445 and Section 3.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated
June 14, 2012.

a. Prior to payment of claims, Management must strictly adhere to

requirements needed on common government transactions to
avoid suspensions and/or disallowances in audit; and
b. Exhaust all available remedies provided under existing laws,
rules and regulations in enforcing immediate settlement of the
disallowances and suspensions pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of
COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

4. Monthly Report of
Accountability for
Accountable Forms must be
regularly submitted for
proper validation, easier
tracking of the used and
unused accountable forms
and avoid possible loss and
misuses of the accountable
forms.

11. We recommended and
urge the Local Chief
Executive to establish a
sound ecological solid
waste management system
in the locality.
12. Constitute the Municipality
Solid Waste Management
(SWM) Boards in pursuant
to Section 2 (par. 2), Rule
VIII and Section 1, Rule VI
of IRR of RA 5447.

14. We recommended that the
Municipal Government to determine their insurable
assets and adequately
cover it with GSIS
insurance to indemnify the
assets in case of
damage/loss due to fire,
earthquake and other
fortuitous event.

15. To strengthen the GAD
Focal Point System of the
LGU, we recommended
that the Management use
the earmarked GAD Funds
not in line with the purposes
of GAD (a) promoting
gender responsive
governance; (b) protecting
and fulfilling women’s
human rights; and (c)
promoting women’s
economic empowerment.
16. Moreover, copy of the
annual accomplishment
report of GAD program,
project and activities vis-à-
vis with target must be
submitted for qualitative
evaluation.

17. We recommended that the
Local Chief Executive
institute the creation of the
Local School Board (LSB) in
his locality and then
required the constituted
LSB for the preparation of
Annual School Budget
Board for purposes of
proper utilization and
disbursement of Special
Education Fund (SEF) in
compliance with Section 98
of RA 7160 and Item Nos.
4 and 5.2 of DEPED-DBM-
DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.

21. We recommended that
management should
initiate measures designed
to come up with a
Memorandum of
Agreement (MOA) with the
GSIS defining therein the
agreed balance of
payables to GSIS, if any,
and the terms and
conditions in the settlement
thereof. The following
steps may help in
facilitating reconciliation of
records.
a) Secure detailed profile
of payables per GSIS
records;
b) Reconstruct old GSIS
accounts, if necessary,
and update records;
c) Reconstruct old
personnel profile, if
necessary, and update
personnel profile;
d) Counter-check GSIS
records with the
reconstructed and
updated accounting
and personnel records
of the LGU;
e) Negotiate with the
GSIS for any
differences that may be
noted during counter-
checking activity;
f) Enter into a formal
agreement with the
GSIS in the form of a
Memorandum of
Agreement (MOA); and
g) Appropriate fund for the
settlement of any
payables to the GSIS
as agreed in the MOA.

22. We recommended that
management must strictly
comply the guidelines
embodied per COA
Circular No. 94-013 on
inter-agency fund transfer
and it must be used only
for the intended purpose it
was allotted.
23. Further, proper accounting
and prompt reporting must
be followed in the booking
of the said fund.

We recommended that Management ensure the payment of casuals/contractual appointments are submitted to CSC and together with other documentary requirements in accordance to Section 4.1.3 of COA Circular No 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The LCE must direct the accountant for the implementation of this recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper reporting and keeping of accounts and maintain the General and subsidiary ledgers not only of revenue accounts but on all accounts presented in the Financial Statements in compliance with Sections 111, 112 and 114 (2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The LCE will convened with the Municipal Accountant to the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the accountant must strictly require proper treatment of travel expenses as cash advances if paid before the date of travel and liquidations and/or reimbursement must be attached with complete supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The accountant must strictly require proper documentation of travel claims in accordance with the guideline.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the accountant must thoroughly process, check the computation of payrolls and require all concerned official and employee to attach the individual daily time record including the other prescribed documentary requirements before payment is made. For effective internal control of handling salaries, it was further recommended that payment of salaries must be done through Automotive Teller Machine (ATM) to lessen the grant of cash advances and avoid the possibility of payroll padding.
observations.managementAction: The LCE will instructed the Accountant for the strict observation of "no DTR no Salary" and thoroughly check the payrolls before processing payments of salaries.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant must immediately submit the paid vouchers and related payrolls pertaining to the remittances made during the year and to establish the amount withheld from salaries of employees and as required per Sec. 100 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Accountant is in the process of reconciling with records from previous years.
observations.reason: Need time to reconcile with records from the previous year.

observations.recommendation: Advice the agency head to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit the required report at soonest time and strictly adhere with Section 100 of P.D. 1445 and Section 3.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Accountant explained that the documents was submitted to their claims of fund from the Provincial Govt. of Lanao del Sur

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the conduct of a thorough and complete physical count of all properties of the municipality. Require the update of inventory reports every end of the year. The accounting and property office must comply with the prescribed policies and procedures in the recording, reporting and maintenance of records of PPE account as well as proper valuation thereof.

We recommended that LGU must be mindful on their targeted development plan in the uses of fund and accomplishment report must be closely monitored and regularly submitted/posted as required for transparency and good governance. They must be aware of the main objective of their development programs and what to focus to achieve a lasting economic, social, political and environmentally developed municipality and uplift the living conditions of their constituents.

Management must keep intact the Requisition and Issue Vouchers including the logbook records wherein all the accountable forms are listed and the name of person/AO who shall be accountable. This is also done for proper booking the value of the accountable forms and accounting the issuances and releases.

We recommended that the Accountable Officer must submit the original copy of the cancelled checks and/or Official Receipt in the Report of Checks Issued and/or Report of Collections and Deposits for proper validation of cancelled forms.

Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms must be regularly submitted for proper validation, easier tracking of the used and unused accountable forms and avoid possible loss and misuses of the accountable forms.

The serial number of checks issued must be reported and arranged in chronological manner in the RCI, Cashbook-Cash in Bank, and Statement of Accountability for Accountable Forms and other reports for proper accounting and easier validation of documents and monitoring the existence of the accountable forms.

We recommend that top management instruct:

4.6.1 The Municipal Treasurer to issue a check with complete supporting documents for the fund transfer of unexpended LDRRM Fund from General Fund to Special Trust Fund, and

4.6.2 The Municipal Account to record the transfer of unexpended LDRRM Fund by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher taking the appropriate accounting entries under General and Trust Fund.

4.6.3 For General Fund books, debit the account Transfer of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds (Account Code 5-02-15-010) and credit the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-01-02-010).

4.6.4 While for Trust Fund books, debit the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-01-02-010) and credit the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code 2-04-01-020).

To achieve the GAD Program objectives and gender mainstream, we recommended that GAD funds must be judicially and efficiently used and further substantially supported with GAD related reports as well as monitoring and evaluation report as in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer continuously effect the timely and accurate mandatory contributions to GSIS, PhilHealth, Pag-ibig, and income tax within the prescribed rate and period of remittances. The discrepancies on the previous year related accounts per financial statement must be thoroughly reviewed and reconcile to present an accurate status of contribution and remittance. Complete employees’ ledger cards must be set-up and maintained for an easier reconciliation and monitoring of the actual status of accounts of LGUs and the employees share.

We recommended that Management exhaust all available remedies provided under existing laws, rules and regulations in enforcing immediate settlement of the disallowances and suspensions pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. It should be emphasized that prior to payment of claims, that management must strictly adhere to COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 for the documentary requirements needed on common government transactions before payment to avoid suspensions and/or disallowances in audit.

We recommended that Management submit the List of PPAs to COA at the onset of the year to promote transparency and good governance and stakeholders was able to monitor the status of the PPAs of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC will coordinate with the COA for the submission of PPA’s at the start of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and urge the Local Chief Executive to establish a sound ecological solid waste management system and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2), Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.
observations.managementAction: The LCE had already instructed the MPDC and the sangguniang Bayan for the creation of the SWM. This was already included an agenda in for the CY 2019 PPA’s.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Government determine their insurable assets and adequately cover it with GSIS insurance to indemnify the assets in case of damage/loss due to fire, earthquake and other fortuitous event.
observations.managementAction: The LCE had instructed the accountant and MPDC to coordinate with GSIS to indemnify the property that will be approved by the GSIS.
observations.reason: Management reasoned the lack of proper documents required by GSIS.

observations.recommendation: To strengthen the GAD Focal Point System of the LGU, we recommended that Management use the earmarked GAD Funds in proper line with the purposes of (a) promoting gender responsive governance; (b) protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights; and (c) promoting women’s economic empowerment. Moreover, copy of the annual accomplishment report of GAD program, project and activities vis-à-vis with target must be submitted for qualitative evaluation.
observations.managementAction: The LCE convened with the GAD focal person for the implementation of GAD programs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive institute the creation of Local School Board (LSB) in his locality and then required the constituted LSB for the preparation of Annual School Budget Board for purposes of proper utilization and disbursement of Special Education Fund (SEF) in compliance with Section 98 of RA 7160 and Item Nos. 4 and 5.2 of DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan must convened for the creation of LSB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management furnish COA copy of any contract/purchase order copy of contracts and PO to COA.
observations.managementAction: The LCE had already instructed the Accountant to always furnished copy of contracts and PO to COA.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal officials must be aware that any financial assistance, contribution and other disbursements to Leagues of Provinces, Municipalities, and Barangays, and Federation of Leagues must be properly accounted, recorded and utilized in accordance to laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The LCE will direct the Accountant and Treasurer to monitor and report any payment for financial assistance, contributions and other disbursements to Leagues of Provinces, Municipalities, Barangays, Federation of Leagues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should initiate measures designed to come up with a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the GSIS defining therein the agreed balance of payables to GSIS, if any, and the terms and conditions in the settlement thereof. The following steps may help in facilitating reconciliation of records: a. Secure detailed profile of payables per GSIS records; b. Reconstruct old GSIS accounts, if necessary, and update records; c. Reconstruct old personnel profile, if necessary, and update personnel profile; d. Counter-check GSIS records with the reconstructed and updated accounting and personnel records of the LGU; e. Negotiate with the GSIS for any differences that may be noted during counter-checking activity; f. Enter into a formal agreement with the GSIS in the form of a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA); and g. Appropriate fund for the settlement of any payables to the GSIS as agreed in the MOA.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented
observations.reason: Reconciliation of the records & accounts with GSIS is still in process before finalizing the Memorandum of Agreement.

observations.recommendation: we recommended that management must strictly comply the guidelines No. 7 on inter-agency fund transfer and it must be used only for the intended purpose it was allotted. Further, proper accounting and prompt reporting must be followed in the booking of the said fund.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented
observations.reason: Reconciliation of the records of the said fund will be worked out and necessary documents will be submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should ensure that due notification to the Auditor of the time and date of every scheduled delivery of goods and services be made on time pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.
observations.managementAction: To be implemented
observations.reason: Mgt. did not offer an explanation to this.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should make sure that observers (consisting of the auditor, a representative of a duly recognized private group in a sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand and a representative of a NGO) are duly notified in writing at least 2-day before the stages of procurement to which observers shall be invited, namely: pre-bid conference; opening of bids; post-qualification; contract award; and special meetings of the BAC.
observations.managementAction: To be implemented
observations.reason: The remoteness of the area and electricity is not stable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should strictly enforce the operational mandates of the Office of the Municipal Assessor in particular to the assessment of real property for the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to come up with a realistic tax valuation of real property for use as valid input in the books and serving as basis in enforcing collections of RPT and SEF.
observations.managementAction: The LCE had meeting with municipal assessor explained the constituents did not consider paying the PRT, they had more pressing needs of spending money on their food.

observations.recommendation: It was recommended that management: • Must initiate to obtain internet access to facilitate internet publication of procurements, such as inviting possible internet providers to install internet services in the community. • Make some pre-arrangements with newspaper media of general nationwide circulation in a way that could facilitate newspaper publications incurring less costs, time and efforts for the LGU, i.e. through phone call, text message or e-mail. • Provide campaign strategies designed at promoting positive image of the Municipality where peace and order is stable and people are hospitable.
observations.managementAction: To be implemented
observations.reason: The remoteness of the area and electricity is not stable.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management: a) Must made sure that the conditions and requirements prescribed in Appendix I of RA 9184 are duly complied with prior to the implementation/construction of any project to be undertaken by administration; b) Must provide campaign strategies designed at promoting positive image of the Municipality where peace and order is stable and people are hospitable; and c) Install safety and security measures so that the life and property of any prospective contractors can properly be safeguarded.
observations.managementAction: To be implemented
observations.reason: Peace and order reasons and the remoteness of the municipality.

For effective internal control of handling salaries, it was further recommended that payment of salaries must be done through Automotive Teller Machine (ATM) to lessen the grant of cash advances and avoid the possibility of payroll padding.

Further, proper accounting and prompt reporting must be followed in the booking of the said fund.

observations.recommendation: Advice the agency head to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit the required report at soonest time and strictly adhere with Section 100 of P.D. 1445 and Section 3.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

observations.recommendation: 1.6    We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the conduct of a thorough and complete physical count of all properties of the municipality. Require the update of inventory reports every end of the year.   The accounting and property office must comply with the prescribed policies and procedures in the recording, reporting and maintenance of records of PPE account.

We recommended that Management ensure payment of casuals/contractual supported with duly verified/accepted accomplishment reports and appointments are submitted to CSC and together with other documentary requirements in accordance to Section 4.1.3 of COA Circular No 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

3.8  We recommended that the Top Management compel the accountable officers and initiate necessary reforms in fiscal reporting and accountability.  The Municipal Accountant must be required to submit promptly the periodic financial and other reports and together with the pertinent supporting documents to the concerned local auditor in accordance to pertinent laws, rules and regulations to ensure timely audit of transactions and verification of accounts.

4.5 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to observe the proper reporting and keeping of accounts and maintain the General and subsidiary ledgers not only on revenue accounts but on all accounts presented in the Financial Statements in compliance with Sections 111, 112 and 114 (2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

5.6 We recommended that Management  widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to include the three priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management.  Rehabilitation of roads which has been on-going since then, must be lessen to give priority to viable projects that could uplift the living condition and provide livelihood to the constituents.

6.5 We recommended that Management follow the LDRRM existing rules and regulations in reporting, booking and utilization of the LDRRM Funds.  The operational structures, system and policies must be strongly in place and followed and applied in case of disaster.  All activities related to mitigation of disaster, starting from preparedness, information dissemination, knowing the extent of damage and number of victims and their needs, procurement and distribution of aid must be well documented and properly reported.

7.5  We recommended that Management submit the List of PPAs to COA at the on-set of the year to promote transparency and good governance and stakeholders were be able to monitor the status of the PPAs of the municipality.

8.6 We recommended and urge the Local Chief Executive to establish a sound ecological solid waste management systems and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2), Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.

9.7  We recommended that Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer continuously effect a timely and accurate mandatory contributions to GSIS, PhilHealth, Pagibig, and income tax within the prescribed rate and period of remittances.  The discrepancies on the previous year related accounts per financial statement must be thoroughly reviewed and reconcile to present an accurate status of contribution and remittance.  Complete employees ledger cards must be set-up and maintained for an easier reconciliation and monitoring of the actual status of accounts of LGUs and the employees share.

11.7 We recommended that Municipal Government determine their insurable assets and adequately cover it with GSIS insurance to indemnify the assets in case of damage/loss due to fire, earthquake and other fortuitous event.

12.5  To strengthen the GAD Focal Point System of the LGU, we recommended that the Management use the earmarked GAD Funds in line with the purposes of (a) promoting gender responsive governance; (b) protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights; and (c) promoting women’s economic empowerment.  Moreover, copy of the annual accomplishment report of GAD program, project and activities vis-à-vis with target must be submitted for qualitative evaluation.

13.7  We recommended that the Local Chief Executive institute the creation of Local School Board (LSB) in his locality and then required the constituted LSB for the preparation of Annual School Budget Board for purposes of proper utilization and disbursement of Special Education Fund (SEF) in compliance with Section 98 of RA 7160 and Item Nos. 4 and 5.2 of DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that Management furnish COA copy of any contract/purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 within 5-days after its perfection/approval/execution for proper evaluation and remedial measures on possible defects will not be too late to enforce.

Required the concerned Municipal Accountant to observe the proper reporting and keeping of accounts and subsidiary records not only on revenue accounts but on all accounts presented in the Financial Statements in compliance with Sections 111 and 114 (2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.  And, source documents must always be preserved for fairness of the presentation of financial statements.  Furthermore, direct him to conduct a thorough inquiry and verification on the circumstances surrounding the existence of the long outstanding Accounts Payable and Inter-agency payables, and their proper disposition as prescribed by applicable COA regulations.
observations.managementAction: The LCE will have meeting with office of the Municipal Accountant and intsructed him to thoroughly  review the  financial statements and observed the prescribed rules in  reporting  the financial operations of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal officials must be aware that any financial assistance, contribution and other disbursements to Leagues of Provinces, Municipalities, Barangays, and Federation of Leagues must be properly accounted, recorded and utilized in accordance to laws, rules and regulations.  Such collective funds from government source involved material amount that if not audited may go astray and subjected to misuse.
observations.managementAction: The LCE will direct the Accountant and Treasurer to monitor and report any payment for finanial assistance, contributions and other disbursements to Leagues of Provinces, Municipalities, Barangays, Federation of Leagues.

observations.reason: Reconciliation of the records and accounts with GSIS is still in process before finalizing the Memorandum of Agreement.  Actual rate of deductions and remittances must be effect for the regular and current personnel of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should make sure that observers (consisting of the concerned auditor, a representative of a duly recognized private group in a sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand and a representative of a NGO) are duly notified in writing at least 2-day before the stages of procurement to which observers shall be invited, namely: pre-bid conference; opening of bids; post-qualification; contract award; and special meetings of the BAC.

We recommended that management should strictly enforce the operational mandates of the Office of the Municipal Assessor in particular to the assessment of real property for the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to come up with a realistic tax valuation of real property for use as valid input in the books and serving as basis in enforcing collections of RPT and SEF receivables.

observations.recommendation:
It was recommended that management:
Must initiate to obtain internet access to facilitate internet publication of procurements, such as inviting possible internet providers to install internet services in the community.  
Make some pre-arrangements with newspaper media of general nationwide circulation in a way that could facilitate newspaper publications incurring less costs, time and efforts for the LGU, i.e. through phone call, text message or e-mail. 
Provide campaign strategies designed at promoting positive image of the Municipality where peace and order is stable and people are hospitable.
observations.reason: The remoteness of the area and electricity is not stable.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that management:
Must made sure that the conditions and requirements prescribed in Appendix I of RA 9184 are duly complied with prior to the implementation/ construction of any project to be undertaken by administration;
Must provide campaign strategies designed at promoting positive image of the Municipality where peace and order is stable and people are hospitable; and
Install safety and security measures so that the life and property of any prospective contractors can properly be safeguarded.
observations.reason: Peace and order reasons and the remoteness of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: we recommended that management must strictly comply the guidelines embodied per COA Circular No. 94-013 on inter-agency fund transfer and it must be used only for the intended purpose it was allotted.  Further, proper accounting and prompt reporting must be followed in the booking of the said fund.
observations.reason: The source documents will be worked out and necessary documents will be submitted.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant must immediately submit the paid vouchers and related payrolls pertaining to the remittances made during the year and to establish the amount withheld from salaries of employees and as required per Sec.  100 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Accountant is in process of reconciling with the financial records from the previous administration.
observations.reason: Need time to reconcile records from previous years.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant must submit the financial reports and supporting documents strictly within the prescribed period required per section 107 of P.D. 1445.
observations.reason: Tone from the top executive.

observations.recommendation: We advise the Municipal Treasurer and his assistant to strictly enforce collectors to remit daily their collections, verify accuracy of their report and prepare the required consolidation report and deposits them intact in pursuance to Section 29 and 33 of the Manual of New Government Accounting System, and submit said repot within five days of the ensuing month.

We recommend that the management should prepare GAD Plan identifying the programs projects and activities to be undertaken that will translate or caters to the demand and commitments in accordance with GAD concerns of the offices to implement the same.  Further, direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to submit an accomplishment report on GAD activities for our review and evaluation.
observations.reason: Although GAD plans was not formally formulated, women’s welfare was among the regular social services provided by health office and the municipal administrator.

observations.recommendation: Required the management to update ledgers on PPE and reconcile with the conduct of periodic inventory on the existence of the assets.
observations.reason: Need time to reconcile records from previous years.

observations.recommendation: Require the agency and the person liable to settle the transactions through the submission of the required explanation/justification and documentation.
observations.managementAction: Accountant will see to it the implementation of this recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management should ensure that the 20% Development fund is optimally and judiciously utilized to help achieve desirable socio-economic and ensure that programs, projects and activities funded are in accordance with the provisions of Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2005, of the DILG and DBM.
observations.reason: MPDC explained that repair of roads is the most pressing needs of the farmers in the remote barangays of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: Advice the agency head to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit the required report at soonest time and strictly adhere with Section 100 of P.D. 1445 and Section 3.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: Accountant explained that the documents was submitted to their claims of fund from the Provincial Govt. of Lanao del Sur

observations.recommendation: Concerned management officials could submit promptly the reports and the disbursement vouchers together with their supporting documents to facilitate the complete review and evaluation and to affirm the reliability on the existence/occurrence, validity, accuracy and completeness of the recorded transactions. Otherwise, appropriate sanction should be imposed on the concerned officers and employees to include suspension of salaries as mandated by Section 122 of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Agency head order responsible officials comply.
observations.reason: Responsible officials lack of training and awareness of regulation on hiring temporary manpower.

observations.recommendation: Direct the accounting office to prepare and submit monthly bank reconciliation statements covering all depository accounts pursuant to Section 74 of P.D. 1445 in order to ascertain the reliability of cash account balance in the financial statement presented.
observations.managementAction: Agency head order responsible officers to comply.

observations.recommendation: 1.5  It is recommended that the management should widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to include the three priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management.  Rehabilitation of roads which has been on-going since then, must be lessen to give priority to viable projects that could uplift the living condition and provide livelihood to the constituents.

2.14 It is recommended that the top management should initiate necessary reforms for fiscal reporting and accountability by:
demonstrating leadership by example;
training personnel to gain work competence and/or hiring additional qualified personnel and or restructuring existing personnel giving the right job for the right person; and
enforcing due compliance with laws, rules and regulations on the rendition of financial reports.
2.15 It is further recommended that top management should direct the concerned municipal accountable officer to submit promptly the period financial reports together with the pertinent supporting documents to the concerned local auditor so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated. Consequently, the top management could also be informed immediately of any deficiencies/errors detected and corrections /adjustments thereof could be effected to their advantage.

3.5 Required the concerned Municipal Accountant to observe the proper reporting and keeping of accounts and subsidiary records not only on revenue accounts but on all accounts presented in the Financial Statements in compliance with Sections 111 and 114 (2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.  And, source documents must always be preserved for fairness of the presentation of financial statements.
3.6 Furthermore, direct him to conduct a thorough inquiry and verification on the circumstances surrounding the existence of the long outstanding Accounts Payable and Inter-agency payables, and their proper disposition as prescribed by applicable COA regulations.

4.6 We recommend that the accountant must thoroughly process, check the computation of payrolls and require all concerned official and employee to attach the individual daily time record including the other prescribed documentary requirements before payment is made.
4.7 For effective internal control of handling salaries, it was further recommended that payment of salaries must be done through Automotive Teller Machine (ATM) to lessen the grant of cash advances and avoid the possibility of payroll padding.

5.6  Encourage and urge the Local Chief Executive to establish a sound ecological solid waste management systems and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2), Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.

6.7  It is recommended that municipal government must determine their insurable assets and adequately cover it with insurance to indemnify the assets in case of damage/loss due to fire, earthquake and other fortuitous event.

7.6  It is recommended that the management should review the deductions made from their employees and rate must be adjusted pursuant to applicable laws, rules and regulations on income tax and GSIS/Pag-ibig/PhilHealth premium contributions.

8.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive must direct the conduct of a thorough and complete physical inventory taking of all properties of the municipality. Require the update of inventory reports of all offices/department every year end which must be further reconcile with the PPE account presented in the financial statement.  Adjustments must be affected to establish the correct balance of PPE per books and the property/equipment ledger card.

9.6  We recommended that Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF must be prepared, certified correct by the Accountant submitted on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Auditor’s Office.  The municipal disaster related activities must be properly documented and monitored for evaluation purpose and readiness of DRRM when time comes. Moreover, any unexpended balance must be maintained on separate account “Trust Liability-DRRM.”

10.6  We recommended that the management must use the earmarked GAD Funds in line with the purposes of (a) promoting gender responsive governance; (b) protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights; and (c) promoting women’s economic empowerment.  Moreover, copy of the annual accomplishment report of GAD program, project and activities vis-à-vis with target must be submitted for qualitative evaluation.

11.4 The Municipal officials must be aware that any financial assistance, contribution and other disbursements to Leagues of Provinces, Municipalities, Barangays, and Federation of Leagues must be properly accounted, recorded and utilized in accordance to laws, rules and regulations.  Such collective funds from government source involved material amount that if not audited may go astray and subjected to misuse.

We recommended the immediate creation of an inventory committee that will conduct a complete physical inventory taking of all properties of the municipality and require update of inventory reports of all offices/department as of December 31, 2015.  We further recommended that the inventory reports must be reconciled with the reported PPE accounts and adjustments must be affected to establish the correct balance per books.
observations.managementAction: Had created an Inventory Committee  per Executive Order 2016-03 as follows:  Chairman-Hon. Dagoroan A. Mindalano, 3 Members - Basit Mindalano; Moh'd. Jorayd Amer; Roemo M. Ali; Naima Ampaso;and Aminollah M. Abdul.  The physical inventory had been initially conducted and reconciliation is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF must be prepared, certified correct by the Accountant submitted on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Auditor’s Office.  Moreover, any unexpended balance must be maintained on separate account “Trust Liability-DRRM.”
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Plan was prepared and regular submission of Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF will be done.

observations.reason: Reconciliation of the records and accounts with GSIS is still in process before finalizing the Memorandum of Agreement.  Actual rate of deductions and remittances must be effect for the regular and current personnel of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the local officials must give priority in the preparation and approval of its Local Government Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan as required under RA 9003.  Earlier plan and awareness on solid waste involving the community will result into a clean and healthy public and environment.
observations.managementAction: Created Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, who will prepare the 10 year Local Solid Waste Management Plan.
The LCE had meeting with the accountable officer for the implementation of this.
The LCE require the accountable officers to attend trainings and they are considering the hiring of competent personnel.
observations.reason: Neophyte graduates and able workers did not consider to work with the municipality due to the remoteness of the area.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should ensure that due notification to the Auditor of the time and date of every scheduled delivery of goods and services be made on time pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.
observations.reason: Mgt. did not offer an explanation to this.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that:
They must be oriented on laws, rules and regulations concerning hiring/employment of contractual workers and strictly adhere thereof. 
Specifically, make sure that claims for wages of casual and contractual workers are duly supported with necessary papers pursuant to COA Circular 2012-001 and other pertinent guidelines.  
Moreover, be guided with the provisions of RA 9184 on the procurement of manpower which provides under Appendix I (3.7) thereof that the manual labor component of projects undertaken by administration may be undertaken (1) in-house (personnel occupying regular plantilla), (2) by job order or through the pakyaw contracting system.
observations.reason: The management did not offer explanation to this.

observations.recommendation:
It was recommended and management agreed that:
Cause for the conduct orientation among concerned personnel on GAD in particular to the provisions of PCW-NEDA-DBM JC 2012-01; 
Specifically, make sure that rules and regulation on proper budgeting, accounting and reporting of GAD Fund are duly complied with; and
GAD Fund must only be utilized according to the intended purpose(s).
observations.managementAction: The personnel assigned had attend GAD and Budget was set-up for this.
observations.reason: The Report will be rightly done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should make sure that observers (consisting of the concerned auditor, a representative of a duly recognized private group in a sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand and a representative of a NGO) are duly notified in writing at least 2-day before the stages of procurement to which observers shall be invited, namely: pre-bid conference; opening of bids; post-qualification; contract award; and special meetings of the BAC.

We recommended that management should strictly enforce the operational mandates of the Office of the Municipal Assessor in particular to the assessment of real property for the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to come up with a realistic tax valuation of real property for use as valid input in the books and serving as basis in enforcing collections of RPT and SEF receivables.
observations.managementAction: The LCE had meeting with municipal assessor.
observations.reason: Municipal assessor explained the constituents did not consider paying the PRT, they had more pressing needs of spending money on their food.
Management said that the fund was set-

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should furnish COA copy of any contract/purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 within 5-day after its perfection/approval/execution.
observations.reason: Management did not offer explanation to this.

observations.recommendation:
It was recommended that management:
Must initiate to obtain internet access to facilitate internet publication of procurements, such as inviting possible internet providers to install internet services in the community.  
Make some pre-arrangements with newspaper media of general nationwide circulation in a way that could facilitate newspaper publications incurring less costs, time and efforts for the LGU, i.e. through phone call, text message or e-mail. 
Provide campaign strategies designed at promoting positive image of the Municipality where peace and order is stable and people are hospitable.
observations.reason: The remoteness of the area and electricity is not stable.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that management:
Must made sure that the conditions and requirements prescribed in Appendix I of RA 9184 are duly complied with prior to the implementation/ construction of any project to be undertaken by administration;
Must provide campaign strategies designed at promoting positive image of the Municipality where peace and order is stable and people are hospitable; and
Install safety and security measures so that the life and property of any prospective contractors can properly be safeguarded.
observations.reason: Peace and order reasons and the remoteness of the municipality.
